name: Prince Mallowheart
description: A frost elf prince and estranged brother of the Cold Prince. Depicted in a bas-relief and in the first mural of the feasting hall beneath Droomen Knoll: a bitter-faced elf in heavy furs, crowned with thorns set with gems. His mural names him Prince Seven-Past-Noon. The chalice retrieved from the frozen pool is said to be his heirloom.
notes: Estranged brother of the Cold Prince.

A bas-relief of his likeness stands behind the frost-coated sundial in the left passage beneath Droomen Knoll. The inscription on the sundial invokes his mercy: "On the slow creep of aeons, Mallow-heart's mercy may shine."
Depicted first in the feasting hall murals, labeled Prince Seven-Past-Noon.
Sentenced Thinwhistle to imprisonment in the cells beneath Droomen Knoll for killing and eating three of his hounds.
The chalice recovered from the frozen pool beneath Droomen Knoll is an heirloom of Mallowheart.
The inscription in the empty cell reads: "Mallowheart be cursed; hound baker Thinwhistle be doubly cursed."
